evaluation_goals: Understand the public scope without reading the private runtime repository.
See the strongest contract, review, and release-evidence surfaces quickly.
Verify that the repo behaves like a maintained OSS contract surface rather than a bulk export.
public_value_claims: The repo publishes a real public contract surface for CapsuleOS and validator-facing artifacts.
The repo exposes both human-readable and machine-readable evidence for external review.
The repo makes scope, portability posture, and published-vs-deferred boundaries explicit.
The repo makes claim-to-evidence traceability explicit instead of leaving reviewers to infer it from scattered files.
The repo makes public example coverage explicit instead of leaving reviewers to guess which fixtures exercise which contracts.
The repo makes public maintainer workflow and release posture explicit instead of relying on implied maintenance behavior.
The repo makes additive, deprecated, and breaking public-change posture explicit instead of leaving change discipline implied across scattered docs.
The repo makes artifact-family ownership and stronger-source hierarchy explicit instead of leaving authority splits implicit across scattered docs.
The repo makes public artifact dependency and reading-order structure explicit instead of forcing reviewers to infer navigation from filenames or folder order.
The repo makes bounded public claims and explicit non-claims reviewable in one machine-readable assurance layer instead of leaving evaluators to infer scope and limits from scattered docs.
The repo makes required co-movement between reviewer, release, contract, and teaching surfaces explicit instead of leaving release sync as maintainer folklore.
The repo makes deferred scope and non-promised behavior explicit instead of leaving reviewers to infer limits from scattered caveat prose.
The repo makes active maintenance and public-surface hardening visible through a bounded machine-readable timeline instead of requiring reviewers to infer evolution from raw git history.
The repo makes bounded external review criteria explicit through a machine-readable review scorecard instead of leaving maturity assessment to raw counts or intuition.
The repo makes verification coverage explicit through a machine-readable matrix instead of hiding trust in one opaque verify command.
The repo makes audience-specific entry paths explicit instead of assuming one generic repo walkthrough fits reviewers, contributors, integrators, tool-builders, and maintainers equally.
The repo makes stronger-source hierarchy explicit instead of forcing reviewers or integrators to guess which public summaries remain subordinate to schemas, OpenAPI, provenance, and release evidence.
The repo makes public adoption readiness explicit instead of leaving outside readers to infer readiness from examples, audience paths, and scattered caveats.
non_claims: This packet does not claim to expose the full private N1Hub runtime.
This packet does not claim that every upstream domain is public.
This packet does not replace the stronger underlying docs, schemas, OpenAPI, or release evidence.
